case_brief: Michael Johnson, a 62-year-old regional sales director at Hartwell Manufacturing, was terminated after 28 years with the company during a 'strategic realignment.' Johnson was the oldest member of a six-person regional director team. All five of the directors retained in the realignment are under 45. Johnson's sales region had the second-highest revenue growth in the prior fiscal year (18%). The company offered all terminated directors a severance package contingent on signing a broad release of all age discrimination claims — an unusual condition that Johnson refused. Internal emails later obtained in discovery contain references to 'bringing fresh energy' and 'new generation leadership' in communications about the realignment. Johnson had received 'outstanding' performance reviews for 25 consecutive years. Jurisdiction: 3rd Circuit (Pennsylvania). Employment at-will but company handbook contains progressive discipline policy which was not followed.
